text:
Admission Date:  1981-03-25              Discharge Date:   1981-03-25

Date of Birth:  1979-09-17             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Biaxin

Attending:Jeffrey
Chief Complaint:
Alcohol withdrawl

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none


History of Present Illness:
Mrs. Bennett is a 55 year old female with a PMH significant
for EtOH abuse with prior DTs and withdrawl seizures, HIV on
HAART, and HCV admitted for EtOH intoxication.  The patient has
a long history of EtOH abuse, although was sober for 3 months
until 3 weeks ago when she started drinking 1 quart of vodka
daily.  Her last drink was yesterday afternoon at 1pm, and
states that she drank 09-16 quart in total.  The patient states
that she has a prior history of withdrawl seizures, most
recently in 04-26 while admitted at Oklahoma Heart Hospital.  Of note,
the patient also reports that she was admitted to Children's National Emergency Department At United Medical Center in 04-23
for EtOH-related aspiration pneumonia.
.
In the Centerstone Of Florida ED, VS 99.6 141/82 95 18 97%RA. She had several
episodes of emesis with associated visual hallucinations
("floaters") as well as feeling "shaky".  The patient received
valium 10 mg po x1 and a total of 3 mg IV ativan for CIWA>10 in
the ED, and was transferred to the MICU for further management.
.
Currently, the patient is resting comfortably.  Denies any
visual, tactile, auditory, or olfactory hallucinations.
Continues to report some shakiness, nausea, chills, sweats,
palpitations, as well as a semi-productive cough x2 weeks.
Denies any fevers, CP, diarrhea, SOB.


Past Medical History:
- HIV - dx 1987.  On long-term antiretroviral treatment
currently with abacavir, atazanavir,Truvada and Ritonavir. 10-26
CD4 571, no VL.
- Hepatitis C - dx 1987.  Seen in liver clinic 02/1990 with no
f/u.  Genotype 1 with HCV VL 3,160,000 copies (03-07).
- EtOH abuse with history of withdrawl seizures
- Fibromyalgia
- Cervical spondylosis
- Colon adenomas
- Arthritis - s/p right total knee arthroplasty 1998-07-28.

Social History:
Disabled since 2010 from HIV. Previously worked as drug
counselor at 0307 Smith Meadow Suite 529
Lindastad, MD 32542.  Divorced, currently living with her
son William Cruz.  Drinks 1 quart vodka daily.  Tobacco - 09-16 ppd x1
year. Remote history of heroin use (20 years ago). Remote
cocaine use - snorting, last several years ago. Recent history
of oxycontin abuse.

Family History:
Positive for cancer in mother, paternal grandmother (breast).
Heart disease in father, mother, and brother, lung disease
father, father also has rheumatoid arthritis.  Mother has
osteoarthritis.

Physical Exam:
Gen: Age appropriate female in NAD
HEENT: Perrl, eomi, sclerae anicteric.  MMM, OP clear without
lesions, exudate, or erythema. Neck supple.
CV: Nl S1+S2, no m/r/g.
Pulm: CTAB
Abd: S/NT/ND, liver edge palpable 2 cm below costal margin,
non-tender. +bs
Ext: No c/c/e. 1+ dp/pt bilaterally
Neuro: AOx3, CN II-XII intact. Mild resting tremor.

Pertinent Results:
1981-03-25 04:15AM BLOOD WBC-7.1 RBC-4.88 Hgb-13.6 Hct-40.9 MCV-84
MCH-27.8 MCHC-33.2 RDW-15.1 Plt Ct-192

1981-03-25 04:15AM BLOOD PT-12.3 PTT-27.8 INR(PT)-1.0

1981-03-25 04:15AM BLOOD Glucose-92 UreaN-8 Creat-0.7 Na-137 K-3.3
Cl-100 HCO3-25 AnGap-15

1981-03-25 04:15AM BLOOD ALT-18 AST-32 LD(LDH)-181 AlkPhos-161*
TotBili-0.8

1981-03-25 04:15AM BLOOD Albumin-3.9 Calcium-8.8 Phos-3.2 Mg-1.6

CXR: pending

Brief Hospital Course:
Mrs. Bennett is a 55 year old female with a PMH significant
for HIV, HCV, arthritis, and seizure disorder admitted for EtOH
withdrawl.  She was admitted directly from the ED to the Aspen Valley Hospital
early in the morning on 09-03 due to history of withdrawal
seizures in the past.  In the ED, she was given both Valium and
Ativan.  On arrival to the floor Mrs. Bennett was monitored
for signs of withdrawal using the CIWA>10 criteria and recieved
2x Valium 5 mg and 3 mg ativan on 09-03.  She was also placed on
continuous IVF at 100 cc/hr with Thiamine and folate.  Mrs.
Bryant was also examined for possible aspiration as evidenced
by cough, CXR was negative.  PO intake was adequate entire stay.
 Mrs. Bennett expressed interest to go to inpatient substance
abuse faility--she was accepted to a program the afternoon of
09-03 and her transfer to Crystal Clinic Orthopaedic Center in 576 Larsen Meadows
South Victoria, CA 33746-2858, MA was arranged that
same day.  At time of transfer, she was hemodynamically stable
with no signs of active alcohol withdrawal.  While admitted,
Mrs.Scott chronic illnesses were managed as below:

HIV: Most recent CD 4 on record 10-26 571 with no HIV VL, HAART
was continued and her PCP was notified.  TMP/SMX was continued
inpatient as patient states she has been on PCP [**Name Initial (PRE) 1102**].

Hepatitis C: Trend LFTs

Seizure disorder: Not currently on seizure prophylaxis.  CIWA as
above.

Arthritis: Dilaudid prn

Medications on Admission:
Abacavir 300 mg po bid
Atazanavir 300 mg po daily
Truvada 200-300 mg, 1 tab po daily
Hydromorphone 2 mg tab, 1 tab Q4H prn for pain
Ritonavir 100 mg cap daily
Loratidine 10 mg daily
TMP/SMX, dose unknown for PCP [**Name Initial (PRE) 1102**].

Discharge Medications:
1. Abacavir 300 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
2. Atazanavir 150 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO DAILY
(Daily).
3. Emtricitabine-Tenofovir 200-300 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet
PO DAILY (Daily).
4. Ritonavir 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO DAILY
(Daily).
5. Multivitamin     Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).

6. Other
Loratidine 10 mg daily


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Discharge Diagnosis:
Primary: alcohol withdrawl
Secondary: HIV, HEPC

Discharge Condition:
stable

Discharge Instructions:
You were admitted for alcohol withdrawl.  You were treated in
the ICU with ativan per withdrawl protocol.  You were also seen
by social work who coordinated your discharge to a treatment
facility.

No new medications were added to your home regimen.  We have
elected not to continue with Bactrim as your PCP's office was
contactWilliam Cruz and stated that you do not currently need to take this
medication prophylactically.

Please return to the emergency room if you should experience
fever >100.4, chest pain, or seizures.

Followup Instructions:
Please follow-up with your PCP, Eric Hall Henry, at Doctors Hospital Llc health on
Mon 05-03 at 4:40 156-886-3128
